positional.bear_case: 9.61% op margin signals real cost/integration (Aerojet) or program-mix pressure; a drawdown to 44.5% of range with deteriorating margins is value-trap territory, and EPS revisions may be drifting negative, removing the positional tailwind entirely.
positional.bull_case: Backlog compounder with multi-year DoD visibility, trading at a real discount on forward earnings; below both MAs after a 20% pullment in a structurally bid defense sector (ITA tailwind) is exactly the entry the archetype rewards over 20d.
positional.falsifier: Latest earnings call showing book-to-bill <0.95 with negative backlog YoY, or 90d EPS revisions turning down >1%, would confirm thesis erosion and flip this positive.

strategic.bear_case: Op margin at 9.61% with supply-chain (titanium/energetics) constraints could cap the re-rating thesis; if DoD budget slips into CR/sequester uncertainty in FY27 negotiations, the defense bid fades and the cohort premium (even if trailing-distorted) reverts.

strategic.falsifier: A confirmed shift in DoD budget phase to sequester/CR uncertainty (failed NDAA, budget caps reinstated) or geopolitical de-escalation toward a peace dividend would flip this firmly positive (trim).

tactical.falsifier: A 10Y move below ~4.0% (or above ~4.7%) within days, producing a clear sympathetic rally or selloff in ENB that creates a tradable tactical edge.

positional.bear_case: 56bps yield spread is genuinely thin; if the 10Y holds at 4.4%+, ENB offers little excess income and the stock can grind sideways-to-down as it has for 24 days, underperforming the book on opportunity cost.
positional.bull_case: WCS-WTI economics and Mainline throughput remain strong, LNG Canada Phase 1 ramping with Phase 2 FID optionality, and a single BoC/Fed cut compresses the required spread instantly — repricing a 4.9% yielder higher fast. The 'rich' signal is purely rate-relative, not balance-sheet driven.
positional.falsifier: 10Y falling toward 4.0% (widening yield spread back above ~100bps) or BoC/Fed initiating a cutting cycle, which would flip this to a buy.

strategic.bear_case: At 25x P/E and a sub-5% yield, ENB is priced for perfection while carrying high leverage in a restrictive-rate world; rising long-end yields or a stalled rate-cut path keep the multiple capped and dividend growth (3-5%) lags the income offered by risk-free bonds.
strategic.bull_case: Enbridge is the premier North American midstream franchise — diversified across liquids, gas transmission, gas utilities and renewables, with a multi-decade secured project backlog and LNG export tailwinds. As BoC/Fed ease, a 4.9% growing, well-covered dividend re-rates and the franchise compounds steadily regardless of near-term richness.
strategic.falsifier: A credible rate-cutting cycle beginning (Fed/BoC) combined with the yield spread widening back above ~150bps, or a major new accretive LNG/gas FID — any of which would flip strategic to a buy.

holdings.key_metric: Yield spread vs 10Y: 56bps (ENB 4.94% − 10Y 4.38%) — rich, well below the 150bps cheap threshold

tactical.bear_case: GLNCY below its 50-day with a falling short-term MA signals real loss of momentum; if copper's pop is a thin-volume head-fake on soft China demand, the gap closes by COPX falling to GLNCY rather than GLNCY rising. Oversold can stay oversold in a downtrend.
tactical.bull_case: Copper surging (COPX +2.26%) with GLNCY lagging is a classic mean-reversion entry; RSI 34 oversold plus a positive day (+0.97%) suggests a bounce is already forming off support near the 200-day.
tactical.falsifier: GLNCY closing decisively below the 200-day ($12.44) on rising volume while COPX rolls over would flip this to a sell.

positional.bear_case: COPX leading could mean GLNCY-specific overhangs (coal ESG discount, cobalt/nickel oversupply weighing on the diversified mix) are keeping it from following copper higher — the spread persists because GLNCY deserves the discount, not because it'll catch up.
positional.bull_case: If copper holds these levels, GLNCY's ~30% copper EBITDA plus a re-rated trading arm could drive a swift catch-up toward the 50-day ($15.21), ~12% upside, with tight credit spreads confirming demand is intact.
positional.falsifier: GLNCY/COPX spread inverting (GLNCY persistently leading) combined with copper breaking down would remove the catch-up thesis and flip the sign.

strategic.bear_case: China property remains structurally weak; if Chinese stimulus disappoints, copper/zinc demand stalls and the 'trough earnings' don't recover — 218x P/E then reflects a value trap, not a spring. P/B 1.62 means there's no replacement-cost margin of safety if NAV gets marked down.
strategic.bull_case: Copper supply is genuinely constrained (grade decline, permitting, Cobre Panama offline) while electrification/AI-datacenter demand inflects; Glencore's copper-heavy book plus a structurally undervalued trading arm (often priced near zero in troughs) offers multi-year re-rating, and buybacks accelerate as the cycle turns.
strategic.falsifier: A sustained copper price breakdown driven by global recession/Chinese demand collapse — pushing the cycle deeper rather than toward recovery — would flip the strategic sign to sell.

holdings.key_metric: GLNCY/COPX spread: -1.29pp (COPX leading, copper surging — GLNCY catch-up setup)

tactical.bear_case: A 52-week low with a -2.4% day is a breakdown, not a bounce — momentum is dominant and BTC can stay oversold for weeks. RSI 32 is not washed out (no <30 capitulation), and a death-cross structure (below both MAs) means rallies get sold. Funding/liquidation cascades could push price into the high-20s before any tradable low.
tactical.bull_case: RSI in low-30s at a fresh range low with strong historical 5-day bounce reliability (100% hit) sets up a sharp mean-reversion rally; crypto oversold snaps are violent and upside is uncapped. Any positive ETF flow print or macro risk-on (VIX 17.65 is benign) could ignite a 10%+ reflex.
tactical.falsifier: A daily close back above the 50DMA (~$40.62) on rising volume, or RSI reclaiming 50, would flip this to a momentum-confirmed regime and remove the oversold-buy edge (sign flip toward neutral/sell).

positional.bear_case: The 4-year cycle may be breaking — a 30%+ drawdown below the 200DMA with decelerating/negative ETF flows and LTH distribution can extend into a true bear leg (2022-style -50%+ from here). My 5.9% positional hit rate is empirical proof that buying these levels has been premature 19 of 20 times. Streak of 12 wrong BUY days argues the trend, not the level, is in control.
positional.bull_case: Spot BTC ETFs structurally absorb supply; a -30.8% dislocation below the 200DMA at a 52w low is historically a generational accumulation zone with uncapped upside. If flows turn positive after this flush, the snapback to the 200DMA (~$48) is a +44% move — exactly what max-conviction positioning captures.
positional.falsifier: ETF flows turning decisively net-positive for a sustained stretch AND price reclaiming the 50DMA would confirm the bottom and validate stronger conviction; conversely, a sustained acceleration of net ETF OUTFLOWS with LTH distribution would flip the sign toward trim/avoid.

strategic.bear_case: If this cycle is genuinely breaking the 4-year pattern and BTC enters a structural sideways/decline regime, month-26 maturity plus a confirmed downtrend means the store-of-value narrative loses momentum and ETF AUM bleeds — a strategic trim would have been right.
strategic.bull_case: Sovereign/institutional adoption and potential regulatory tailwinds (clearer US crypto framework, possible strategic reserve discussions) extend the cycle far beyond the historical 4-year template; the strategic case is uncapped and a drawdown here is a long-term entry, not a maturity warning.
strategic.falsifier: Evidence the secular adoption curve is reversing — sustained multi-quarter ETF AUM decline plus regulatory rollback — would flip strategic to a clear trim; conversely a major positive regulatory/sovereign catalyst would push strategic negative.

holdings.key_metric: BTC vs 200DMA: -30.8% (price $33.36 vs 200DMA $48.20), at fresh 52-week low

tactical.bear_case: MXN has been quietly strong all year; if a US risk-off pulse hits (VIX 17.65 is benign but can spike), EM ADRs like KOF give back fast on peso weakness, and the +0.67% slip yesterday hints at fading momentum near the SMA50.
tactical.bull_case: Holding above both MAs with RSI mid-range gives KOF room to grind higher without being overbought; a stable peso and defensive staples bid could carry it back toward 52w highs as a low-beta EM haven.
tactical.falsifier: A single-day MXN move beyond ~18.0 or below ~16.8, or RSI breaking <40 or >70, would create a directional tactical edge and flip this off zero.

positional.bear_case: Mexican consumer volumes face pressure from elevated Banxico rates (still restrictive) and any nearshoring slowdown; KOF's ~60% Mexico mix means a domestic consumption soft patch hits volumes while PET/sugar input costs stay sticky, compressing the multiple from here.
positional.bull_case: KOF is a best-in-class bottler executing on pricing/mix with disciplined capital returns; a Banxico easing cycle would boost Mexican consumer purchasing power and the peso simultaneously, lifting both volumes and the USD-translated ADR.
positional.falsifier: A break back below the SMA200 (~$97.8) on volume, or a quarterly volume/margin miss, would turn the positional setup negative; conversely a clear Banxico dovish pivot with peso strength would push it firmly buy-side.

strategic.bear_case: Mexico carries structural political/policy risk under the Sheinbaum administration and ongoing US-Mexico trade/tariff friction; a sustained peso devaluation regime (toward 19-20) would erode USD returns for years regardless of operational quality, and EM equity risk premium could re-rate higher.
strategic.bull_case: At 12.8x with a growing dividend, KOF is structurally undervalued versus developed-market staples peers (20x+); nearshoring is a multi-year tailwind for Mexican formal employment and consumption, and continued FX stability plus dividend compounding could deliver a re-rating toward mid-teens P/E.
strategic.falsifier: A durable MXN break above ~19 (devaluation regime) or a US tariff regime specifically targeting Mexican trade would flip the strategic case positive; a Banxico easing cycle with sustained peso strength below ~16.5 would deepen the buy case.

holdings.key_metric: Trailing P/E: 12.8x (value territory for a staples compounder yielding 3.76%)

tactical.bear_case: Oversold can persist; a fresh Lula/CEO/fuel-pricing headline or dividend cut announcement could push PBR.A through SMA200 ($15.68 already broken to downside) with no mean reversion, exactly the pattern that produced the 28.6% historical hit rate.
tactical.bull_case: RSI 25.76 with WTI holding $79 and BRL stable at 5.16 sets up a sharp technical snapback; any benign political news or dividend reaffirmation could trigger a fast 5-8% recovery from deeply oversold levels.
tactical.falsifier: RSI recovering above 40 with price reclaiming a higher low, or conversely a fresh negative political headline driving a new low below $14.20 on volume — either would flip the tactical read.

positional.bear_case: A 23.7% gap below SMA50 typically signals a damaged trend, not a dip. If Lula forces refining capex or pressures fuel pricing, the positional downtrend extends and the 9.4% yield gets re-rated lower as payout risk rises.
positional.bull_case: At 4.8x P/E and 9.4% yield with WTI near $79 and pre-salt breakeven ~$35, PBR.A is a deep-value cash machine; mean-reversion to SMA50 ($19) implies ~30% upside if political fears prove overblown and dividends hold.
positional.falsifier: Board reaffirmation of dividend policy plus production growth guidance would confirm the buy; a dividend cut or forced capex mandate would flip to trim.

strategic.bear_case: 4.8x P/E flags peak earnings; if WTI softens on OPEC+ supply additions and weak China demand, PBR earnings and the special dividends roll over, while Lula-era forced capex erodes the very free cash flow underpinning the thesis.
strategic.bull_case: Pre-salt low breakevens (~$35) and disciplined production growth mean Petrobras compounds cash through-cycle; a 9.4% base yield plus extraordinaries at a 4.8x multiple is a structurally cheap EM energy cash engine that re-rates if political fears fade.
strategic.falsifier: WTI breaking decisively below ~$65 (deepening earnings rollover) confirms trim; a sustained oil rally above $90 with dividend policy intact would flip strategic to buy.

holdings.key_metric: Trailing P/E (inverted-cyclical read): 4.8x — signals PEAK earnings / cycle-rollover risk while dividend yield sits at 9.4%

tactical.bear_case: The 3.31% single-day jump is a low-conviction ADR-liquidity blip with no confirming volume; freight spot rates (Drewry WCI) have been softening off Q1 highs, so the rally fades and RSI rolls back below 45 within a week.
tactical.bull_case: Renewed Red Sea attack headlines or a fresh round of Asia-Europe rate hikes (GRIs) reignite spot rates, momentum carries AMKBY through the SMA50 cleanly toward the prior swing high.
tactical.falsifier: Drewry WCI prints two consecutive weekly declines >5% AND price closes back below SMA50 ($12.13) — that flips tactical to trim-side positive.

positional.bear_case: Spot rate normalization as Red Sea reroute capacity gets absorbed and new-build deliveries (record orderbook ~20%+ of fleet) hit the water in 2H, compressing the rate environment and pulling price back to SMA200 ($11.52).
positional.bull_case: Suez disruption persists longer than consensus expects, extended tonne-mile demand sustains contract rate resets higher, and the golden-cross trend continues to grind up with the broad freight complex.
positional.falsifier: Price closes below SMA200 ($11.52) on rising volume, breaking the golden-cross structure — flips positional to positive/trim.

strategic.bear_case: The record industry orderbook (~20%+ of global fleet) delivering into 2025-26 structurally caps rates for years; book value itself gets impaired as vessel values fall, so 0.70x P/B is not as cheap as it looks and earnings normalize toward zero/loss.
strategic.bull_case: P/B 0.70 below replacement cost limits downside; decarbonization-driven fleet renewal + IMO scrapping tightens effective supply; Logistics segment (Hummingbird/integrator strategy) re-rates toward freight-forwarder peer multiples, unlocking sum-of-parts value the market currently prices at zero.
strategic.falsifier: Maersk guides Ocean EBIT to structural losses for FY26 AND announces a buyback suspension — that removes the value floor and flips strategic positive.

holdings.key_metric: Price / Book: 0.70x — below book and near replacement cost for an asset-heavy fleet
